name: Asian Counseling and Referral Service
notes: For outpatient mental health services ages 6 and up, call CHS Centralized Screening at 206-362-7282 Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Have your Medicaid/Apple Health or insurance number ready. You may be given an assessment appointment or open access hours depending on the location. CHS says these assessments do not meet requirements for competency or parenting plan evaluations.

name: Asian Counseling and Referral Service
address: 3639 Martin Luther King Jr Way S, Seattle, WA 98144
notes: Substance use services for adults only.

name: Consejo Counseling and Referral Services
address: 12360 Lake City Way NE, Seattle, WA 98125
notes: Contact the Lake City clinic directly at 206-461-4880 or visit consejocounseling.org to get started. Open Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Offers mental health outpatient services.

name: Consejo Counseling and Referral Services
notes: Contact the Bellevue clinic directly at 425-679-0801 or visit consejocounseling.org to get started. Open Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Offers mental health outpatient services, domestic violence survivor services, children, youth, and family services, and transitional housing.

name: Consejo Counseling and Referral Services
notes: Contact the Renton clinic directly at 206-461-4880 or visit consejocounseling.org to get started. Consejo says the next step is a no-cost initial consultation to review eligibility, understand your needs, and determine how they can best support you. Open Monday through Friday, 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM.

name: Eating Recovery Center of Washington
notes: Eating disorders only. By referral only.

notes: REACH program. Call ahead.

name: Ryther
notes: New clients can call Ryther at 206-517-0234 Monday through Friday, 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM Pacific, or use Ryther's General Intake Form. If you have trouble using the form or prefer to speak with the intake team, call 206-517-0234. Ryther says to allow up to 5 business days for contact.

name: SeaMar Behavioral Health
notes: Infant mental health, youth and family services.

name: Seneca Family of Agencies
notes: Youth and family services only.

name: Sound
address: 11000 Lake City Way NE, Seattle, WA 98125
notes: You can fill out Sound's online form at sound.health/get-care/client-resources, walk into a clinic during walk-in hours for a same-day screening, or call the Admissions Team at 206-901-2000. Bring what you can, such as a photo ID, insurance card, contact information for other providers, or a medication list, but these are not required to receive care. Staff may ask about current or past treatment and insurance, including Medicaid/Apple Health, Medicare, private insurance, or no insurance. Sound says no one is turned away from established services due to inability to pay, and sliding scale support may be available.

name: Valley Cities Counseling and Consultation
notes: Call first to speak with a screener at (253) 833-7444. Intake support is available Monday through Thursday, 8:30 AM to 6:30 PM, and Friday, 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM. In-person intake assessments are available at neighborhood clinics; call ahead for availability. You can ask for an interpreter or other accommodations when you call. Substance use services are for adults only.
